summary:
The woman needs urgent treatment due to severe symptoms suggesting serious toxicity; immediate steps include stabilizing circulation via fluids and monitoring/respiratory support. Administering large doses of atropine before confirming need may mask other issues thus is inappropriate among available choices. To summarize concisely focusing only on core aspects related directly addressing the query posed,

The primary focus must remain on rapidly stabilizing circulatory function through appropriate interventions like fluid therapy and ensuring adequate ventilation despite no clear indication yet exists supporting pre-emptive use of high-dose atropine absent definitive diagnosis indicating necessity thereof. Hence, administering substantial amounts of atropine prior to confirmation does not fit best course action amongst presented possibilities considering current knowledge base provided. 

Key Points Summed Up In Two Sentences For Clarity And Precision:

Rapidly stabilize cardiovascular status using intravenous fluids & secure airways promptly but avoid unnecessary aggressive pharmacological intervention until diagnostic clarity improves significantly especially avoiding excessive initial application of drugs like atropine lacking strong supportive rationale currently present.
